other_observers: Brenda Wright, Ron Lambeth, Vic Zerbi, Lee Stigen, Bill Day, Carol Brusky, Ronda Woodward, Jeff Tischbien, Peter Robinson, Dave Price and Steve Watson.; Dave Leatherman found the bird the evening of the 1st. Several birders came out that evening a viewed the jaeger. There are many more photos of the bird that may be better than those that Brenda took.
specific_location: Whitewater south of Grand Jct 5 miles on CO-141 1/2 mile west of the Gunnison River Bridge.
description: The bird was gull-like in appearance about the size of a small Ring-billed Gull. When in flight the wings were brown both above and below and the color of the back. I did not see any white wing patches. Flight was somewhat tern like. The belly was light gray and graded into a very light chest and neck color. The bird had a dark gray cap. In flight a very light chest band was visible only at the shoulders. Black and white barring was visible on the undertail coverts and slightly visible under the wings. The tail had short, rounded, central feathers the were visible in flight. The bill was not stout, dark and somewhat long. The jaeger was a light phase bird.Behaviors: The jaeger would fly around and them float on the water for a rest. It would then fly around some more.Call: none heardPlumage: Adult nonbreeding
photographer: Photographer: Brenda Wright, Coen Dexter, Coen Dexter
